comment_text.sn1.7:2.1: Paṭividita (“deciphered”) is found only here and DN 1:1.3.2, where it is likewise in the context of understanding views.

root_text.sn1.7:1.2: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho sā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:

translation_text.sn1.7:1.2: Standing to one side, that deity spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.7:2.2: may be led astray by the doctrines of others.
translation_text.sn1.7:2.3: Asleep, they have not woken up:
translation_text.sn1.7:2.4: it is time for them to wake!”
translation_text.sn1.7:3.1: “Those who have well deciphered the teachings
translation_text.sn1.7:3.2: won’t be led astray by the doctrines of others.
translation_text.sn1.7:3.3: They have woken up, they rightly know,
translation_text.sn1.7:3.4: smoothly they walk in the rough.”

reference_text.sn1.7:1.1: bj13.8, csp1ed12.4, csp2ed12.4, dr15.5, ms12S1_22, msdiv7, pts-vp-pli1ed1.4, pts-vp-pli2ed1.7
